title: New Advances Bring Hope in the Fight Against Cancer
subtitle: From smart pills to better surgery, researchers are finding new ways to treat and prevent the world's deadliest diseases.

summary.en: Recent medical conferences and studies have revealed major progress in cancer treatment. Scientists have developed new smart drugs that help the immune system better identify and destroy cancer cells. A new pill for pancreatic cancer has shown the ability to double survival times for patients. Surgical research suggests that choosing the right method can significantly lower the risk of cervical cancer returning. However, experts warn that a global shortage of healthcare workers could create future challenges for cancer care.
summary.zh: 近期的醫學會議與研究揭示了癌症治療的重大進展。科學家已研發出新型智慧藥物，能協助免疫系統更有效地識別並摧毀癌細胞。一種針對胰臟癌的新藥已展現出能將患者存活時間延長一倍的能力。外科研究顯示，選擇正確的手術方法能顯著降低子宮頸癌復發的風險。然而，專家警告，全球醫療人力短缺可能為未來的癌症照護帶來挑戰。

paragraphs.en: Medical experts are reporting significant progress in the global fight against cancer. At a recent conference in Chicago, researchers shared new findings on "smart drugs" and treatments that could change how doctors manage some of the most difficult cases. These developments offer fresh hope to patients who previously had few options left.
paragraphs.zh: 醫學專家指出，全球對抗癌症的戰役已取得重大進展。在芝加哥近期舉行的一場會議中，研究人員分享了關於「智慧藥物」與治療方式的最新發現，這些成果可能改變醫師處理部分最棘手病例的方式。這些發展為過去幾乎別無選擇的患者帶來了新的希望。

paragraphs.en: One of the most exciting areas of research involves immunotherapy, which is a type of treatment that uses the body’s own immune system to fight cancer. While these drugs have changed cancer care over the last ten years, they do not work for everyone because some cancer cells are able to hide from the immune system. According to The Guardian Health, scientists have created a new experimental tablet called GRWD5769. This drug acts like a key, removing the "invisibility cloaks" that cancer cells use to hide. Once these cells are exposed, the immune system can find and destroy them. In a study involving patients with several types of cancer, including lung and liver cancer, the drug helped shrink tumours in many participants.
paragraphs.zh: 研究領域中最令人振奮的方向之一是免疫療法，這是一種利用人體自身免疫系統來對抗癌症的治療方式。雖然這類藥物在過去十年中改變了癌症照護，但並非對每個人都有效，因為有些癌細胞能夠躲避免疫系統的偵測。根據《衛報健康》（The Guardian Health）報導，科學家研發出一種名為 GRWD5769 的新型實驗性藥錠。這種藥物就像一把鑰匙，能移除癌細胞用來隱身的「隱形斗篷」。一旦這些細胞暴露出來，免疫系統就能發現並摧毀它們。在一項針對多種癌症（包括肺癌與肝癌）患者的研究中，該藥物協助許多參與者的腫瘤縮小。

paragraphs.en: Another major breakthrough presented in Chicago involves pancreatic cancer, which is often considered the world’s deadliest common cancer. A new pill called daraxonrasib has shown remarkable results. In a trial of 500 patients, those who took the drug lived for an average of 13.2 months, compared to about 6.6 months for those who received standard chemotherapy. Dr. Rachna Shroff, an expert at the University of Arizona Cancer Center, described these results as "landscape-changing," noting that this discovery is one of the most important in decades.
paragraphs.zh: 在芝加哥發表的另一項重大突破與胰臟癌有關，胰臟癌常被視為全球最致命的常見癌症。一種名為 daraxonrasib 的新藥展現了顯著的成效。在一項五百名患者的臨床試驗中，服用該藥物的患者平均存活時間為十三點二個月，而接受標準化學治療的患者平均存活時間僅約六點六個月。亞利桑那大學癌症中心（University of Arizona Cancer Center）的專家拉赫娜·史洛夫（Rachna Shroff）醫師形容這些結果「改變了整體局勢」，並指出這是數十年來最重要的發現之一。

paragraphs.en: Beyond new drugs, researchers are also looking at how surgical choices affect long-term health. A study published in JAMA Surgery and reported by MedPage Today compared two common ways to treat cervical lesions, which are abnormal cells that can lead to cancer. The study looked at data from Sweden and China and found that a procedure called cold knife conization (CKC) was more effective than the more common loop electrosurgical excision procedure (LEEP). Patients who had the CKC procedure had a lower risk of the disease coming back. The researchers suggested that doctors should carefully choose the surgical method based on the patient's specific needs and the tools available at their hospital.
paragraphs.zh: 除了新藥之外，研究人員也正在探討手術選擇如何影響長期健康。《美國醫學會外科期刊》（JAMA Surgery）發表並經由《MedPage Today》報導的一項研究，比較了兩種治療子宮頸病變（可能導致癌症的異常細胞）的常見方式。該研究檢視了來自瑞典與中國的數據，發現一種稱為「冷刀錐狀切除術」（cold knife conization, CKC）的程序，比更常見的「子宮頸環狀電切術」（loop electrosurgical excision procedure, LEEP）更有效。接受冷刀錐狀切除術的患者，疾病復發的風險較低。研究人員建議，醫師應根據患者的具體需求與醫院現有的設備，謹慎選擇手術方法。

paragraphs.en: While these scientific advances are promising, the medical community is also facing serious challenges. A report from The Lancet Oncology Commission, cited by MedPage Today, describes a "global crisis" regarding the number of people available to provide care. The report warns that the world could face a shortage of 100 million cancer care positions over the next 25 years. This shortage could make it difficult for patients to access the new treatments being developed today.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管這些科學進展令人期待，醫學界也正面臨嚴峻挑戰。《刺胳針腫瘤學委員會》（The Lancet Oncology Commission）的一份報告經由《MedPage Today》引用，描述了關於醫療照護人力資源的「全球危機」。報告警告，未來二十五年內，全球可能面臨一億個癌症照護職位的缺口。這種短缺可能導致患者難以取得現今研發出的新療法。

paragraphs.en: Technology is also playing a larger role in modern medicine. According to MedPage Today, artificial intelligence (AI) is already helping doctors manage their busy schedules and analyze medical images more accurately. For example, AI programs used for mammogram analysis have helped doctors identify high-risk patients more quickly. Additionally, other research is exploring how existing drugs, such as aspirin, might help detect diseases like bladder cancer earlier than current methods allow.
paragraphs.zh: 科技在現代醫學中也扮演著日益重要的角色。根據《MedPage Today》報導，人工智慧（AI）已在協助醫師管理繁忙的行程，並更精確地分析醫學影像。例如，用於乳房攝影分析的人工智慧程式，已協助醫師更快速地識別高風險患者。此外，其他研究也正在探討現有藥物（如阿斯匹靈）是否能比現行方法更早偵測出膀胱癌等疾病。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the combination of new drugs, better surgical techniques, and improved technology offers a brighter future for cancer patients. Prof. Fiona Thistlethwaite, who led the trial for the new smart drug GRWD5769, noted that while these are still early days, the results are very impressive. As researchers continue to test these new methods, the goal remains the same: to make cancer treatments more effective, less harmful, and more widely available to everyone who needs them.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，新藥、更佳的手術技術與改良科技的結合，為癌症患者帶來了更光明的未來。帶領新型智慧藥物 GRWD5769 臨床試驗的費歐娜·希斯索斯韋特（Fiona Thistlethwaite）教授指出，雖然目前仍處於早期階段，但結果非常令人印象深刻。隨著研究人員持續測試這些新方法，目標始終如一：讓癌症治療更有效、副作用更小，並讓所有需要的人都能更廣泛地獲得治療。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To treat or control a medical condition.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作「管理」，這裡指醫療上的控制病情。文中：treatments that could change how doctors manage some of the most difficult cases.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To return or recur, specifically referring to a disease or symptom.
keyTerms.noteZh: 這裡指疾病再次出現。文中：Patients who had the CKC procedure had a lower risk of the disease coming back.

keyTerms.definitionEn: A stage in a process where something has only just begun.
keyTerms.noteZh: 指某事物剛開始的階段。文中：Prof. Fiona Thistlethwaite, who led the trial for the new smart drug GRWD5769, noted that while these are still early days, the results are very impressive.

sources.name: The Guardian Health
sources.title: Removing ‘invisibility cloaks’ and safely skipping chemo: new weapons in war on cancer shared at US conference

sources.name: MedPage Today
sources.title: One Surgical Technique for Cervical Lesions May Reduce Recurrences

sources.name: MedPage Today
sources.title: Aspirin and Bladder Cancer; Oncology Workforce Shortage; 'Exploding Immune Cells'

quiz.question: According to the study on pancreatic cancer, what was the primary difference in outcomes between patients taking daraxonrasib and those receiving standard chemotherapy?

options.A: Patients taking the new drug experienced fewer side effects than those on chemotherapy.
options.B: Patients taking the new drug lived for twice as long on average as those on chemotherapy.
options.C: Patients on chemotherapy had a higher rate of tumor shrinkage than those taking the new drug.
options.D: The study found no significant difference in survival rates between the two groups.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第三段明確指出，服用 daraxonrasib 的患者平均存活時間為 13.2 個月，而接受標準化療的患者僅為 6.6 個月，13.2 正好是 6.6 的兩倍。選項 A 文章未提及副作用比較；選項 C 與文章提到的療效相反；選項 D 則與數據顯示的顯著差異不符。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the future accessibility of the new cancer treatments mentioned in the article?

options.A: They will likely become cheaper as AI technology continues to improve.
options.B: They may remain out of reach for many patients due to a lack of medical personnel.
options.C: They are expected to replace all traditional surgical methods within the next decade.
options.D: They will be immediately available to all patients globally regardless of location.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章第五段引用《The Lancet Oncology Commission》的報告，指出未來 25 年內全球將面臨 1 億個癌症照護職位的短缺，這直接導致患者可能難以獲得這些新療法。選項 A 文章未提及成本降低；選項 C 過度推論，文章僅提到手術方式的優化而非全面取代；選項 D 與文中提到的醫療人力危機矛盾。

quiz.question: In the third paragraph, what does Dr. Rachna Shroff mean when she describes the results of the daraxonrasib trial as 'landscape-changing'?

options.A: The results have physically altered the appearance of the hospital environment.
options.B: The results have significantly improved the accuracy of medical imaging software.
options.C: The results have fundamentally transformed the existing standards of cancer treatment.
options.D: The results have caused researchers to change the physical location of their study.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。「landscape-changing」在此處為隱喻用法，指該發現具有開創性，徹底改變了該領域的現狀或標準。選項 A 是對「landscape」的字面誤解；選項 B 混淆了文章後段關於 AI 的描述；選項 D 則是對詞彙的錯誤解讀，與地理位置無關。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article regarding the current state of cancer research?

options.A: While medical technology is advancing, surgery remains the only reliable way to treat cancer.
options.B: The primary focus of modern medicine is now entirely on replacing human doctors with AI.
options.C: New developments offer significant promise, but the field faces major challenges in implementation.
options.D: Cancer research has reached a point where most types of the disease can now be cured easily.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章開頭提到醫學進展帶來新希望，但隨後在第五段轉折，強調了醫療人力短缺的「全球危機」，顯示儘管科技進步，實務執行仍面臨困難。選項 A 忽略了藥物治療的貢獻；選項 B 過度誇大 AI 的角色；選項 D 錯誤解讀文章，文中僅稱進展「令人印象深刻」且處於「早期階段」，並未宣稱癌症已能輕易治癒。

aiDisclaimer: 本文由 AI 綜合多家報導生成，事實請以原始來源為準。
